exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n40\t    pass\n41\t\n42\t\n43\tclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n44\t    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n45\t    pass\n46\t\n47\t\n48\tclass unreachable.\"\"\"\n55\t    pass\n56\t\n57\t\n58\tclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n59\t    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n60\t\n61\t    def retry_count\n65\t        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n66\t\n67\t\n68\tclass IntentType(Enum):\n69\t    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n70\t    STATUS = \"status\"\n71\t    ACTION = \"action\"\n72\t    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n73\t    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n74\t    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n75\t    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n76\t    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n77\t    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n78\t    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n79\t    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n80\t\n81\t\n82\t@dataclass\n83\tclass IntentClassification:\n84\t    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n85\t    intent_type: IntentType\n86\t    project_slug: str | None = None\n87\t    confidence: float = 1.0\n88\t    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n89\t    reasoning: str = \"\"\n90\t    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n91\t    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n92\t\n93\t\n94\t@dataclass\n95\tclass RoutedIntent:\n96\t    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n97\t    intent_id: str\n98\t    classification: IntentClassification\n99\t    session_id: str\n100\t    utterance: str\n101\t    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n102\t    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n103\t    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n104\t    router_ms: int | None = None\n105\t\n106\t\n107\t# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n108\t# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n109\t# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\n110\tROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n111\t\n112\t# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n113\t_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n114\t    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n115\t    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n116\t    \"intent objects.\"\n117\t)\n118\t\n119\t\n120\tclass IntentRouter:\n121\t    \"\"\"\n122\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n123\t\n124\t    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n125\t    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n126\t    \"\"\"\n127\t\n128\t    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n129\t        self.store = store\n130\t        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n131\t        self._zai_client = None\n132\t        self._reload_manager = None\n133\t\n134\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n135\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n136\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n137\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n138\t        return self._zai_client\n139\t\n140\t    async def _get_store(self):\n141\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n142\t        if self.store is None:\n143\t            self.store = get_store()\n144\t        return self.store\n145\t\n146\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n147\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n148\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n149\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n150\t        return self._reload_manager\n151\t\n152\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n153\t        \"\"\"\n154\t        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n155\t\n156\t        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n157\t        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n158\t        \"\"\"\n159\t        try:\n160\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n161\t        except Exception as e:\n162\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n163\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n164\t\n165\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n166\t        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n167\t\n168\t        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n169\t        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n170\t        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n171\t        \"\"\"\n172\t        return self._load_router_prompt()\n173\t\n174\t    async def classify_utterance(\n175\t        self,\n176\t        utterance: str,\n177\t        session_id: str,\n178\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n179\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n180\t        \"\"\"\n181\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n182\t\n183\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n184\t\n185\t        Args:\n186\t            utterance: The user utterance\n187\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n188\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n189\t\n190\t        Returns:\n191\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n192\t\n193\t        Raises:\n194\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n195\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n196\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n197\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n198\t        \"\"\"\n199\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n200\t\n201\t        # Build user message\n202\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n203\t\n204\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n205\t\n206\t        # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n207\t        # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n208\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n209\t\n210\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n211\t        retry_count = 0\n212\t        raw_response = None\n213\t\n214\t        try:\n215\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time\n216\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n217\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n218\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n219\t                user_message=user_message,\n220\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n221\t                max_tokens=256,\n222\t                temperature=0.0,\n223\t            )\n224\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n225\t\n226\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n227\t            raw_response = response\n228\t\n229\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n230\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n231\t            try:\n232\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n233\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n234\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n235\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n236\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n237\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n238\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n239\t                raise e\n240\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n241\t\n242\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n243\t            logger.info(\n244\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n245\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n246\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n247\t                f\"total_estimate_ms={proxy_ms + parse_ms:.2f}\"\n248\t            )\n249\t            classifications = []\n250\t\n251\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n252\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n253\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n254\t                try:\n255\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n256\t                except ValueError:\n257\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n258\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n259\t\n260\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n261\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n262\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n263\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n264\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n265\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n266\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n267\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n268\t                )\n269\t                classifications.append(classification)\n270\t\n271\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n272\t            return classifications\n273\t\n274\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n275\t            \"\"\"\n276\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n277\t\n278\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n279\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n280\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n281\t            \"\"\"\n282\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n283\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n284\t\n285\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n286\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n287\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n288\t                retry_count += 1\n289\t                try:\n290\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n291\t                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n292\t                        utterance=utterance,\n293\t                        session_id=session_id,\n294\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n295\t                    )\n296\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n297\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n298\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n299\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n300\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n301\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n302\t                    ) from retry_e\n303\t\n304\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n305\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n306\t                parse_error=str(e),\n307\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n308\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n309\t            ) from e\n310\t\n311\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n312\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n313\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n314\t\n315\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n316\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n317\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n318\t\n319\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n320\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n321\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n322\t\n323\t        except Exception as e:\n324\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n325\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n326\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n327\t\n328\t    async def route_utterance(\n329\t        self,\n330\t        utterance: str,\n331\t        utterance_id: str,\n332\t        session_id: str,\n333\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n334\t        \"\"\"\n335\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n336\t\n337\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n338\t\n339\t        Args:\n340\t            utterance: The user utterance\n341\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n342\t            session_id: Session ID\n343\t\n344\t        Returns:\n345\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n346\t\n347\t        Raises:\n348\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n349\t        \"\"\"\n350\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n351\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n352\t\n353\t        try:\n354\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n355\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n356\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n357\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n358\t            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n359\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n360\t\n361\t            routed_intents = []\n362\t            for classification in classifications:\n363\t                # Create intent ID\n364\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n365\t\n366\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n367\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n368\t                    classification=classification,\n369\t                    session_id=session_id,\n370\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n371\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n372\t                )\n373\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n374\t\n375\t            return routed_intents\n376\t\n377\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n378\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n379\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n380\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n381\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n382\t                utterance=utterance,\n383\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n384\t                session_id=session_id,\n385\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n386\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n387\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n388\t            )\n389\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n390\t\n391\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n392\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n393\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n394\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n395\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n396\t                utterance=utterance,\n397\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n398\t                session_id=session_id,\n399\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n400\t            )\n401\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n402\t\n403\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n404\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n405\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n406\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n407\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n408\t                utterance=utterance,\n409\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n410\t                session_id=session_id,\n411\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n412\t            )\n413\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n414\t\n415\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n416\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n417\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n418\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n419\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n420\t                utterance=utterance,\n421\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n422\t                session_id=session_id,\n423\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n424\t            )\n425\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n426\t\n427\t    async def process_intent(\n428\t        self,\n429\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n430\t    ) -> dict:\n431\t        \"\"\"\n432\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n433\t\n434\t        Args:\n435\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n436\t\n437\t        Returns:\n438\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n439\t        \"\"\"\n440\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n441\t\n442\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n443\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n444\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n445\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n446\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n447\t        # the dispatch itself.\n448\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n449\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n450\t\n451\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n452\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n453\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n454\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n455\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n456\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n457\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n458\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n459\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n460\t            )\n461\t\n462\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n463\t            store = await self._get_store()\n464\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n465\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n466\t                \"resolved\",\n467\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n468\t            )\n469\t\n470\t            return {\n471\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n472\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n473\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n474\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n475\t            }\n476\t\n477\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n478\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n479\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n480\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n481\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n482\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n483\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n484\t            )\n485\t\n486\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n487\t            store = await self._get_store()\n488\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n489\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n490\t                \"resolved\",\n491\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n492\t            )\n493\t\n494\t            return {\n495\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n496\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n497\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n498\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n499\t            }\n500\t\n501\t        try:\n502\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n503\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n504\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n505\t            else:\n506\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n507\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n508\t        except Exception:\n509\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n510\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n511\t            raise\n512\t\n513\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n514\t        return result\n515\t\n516\t    async def _persist_timings(\n517\t        self,\n518\t        intent_id: str,\n519\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n520\t    ) -> None:\n521\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n522\t        try:\n523\t            store = await self._get_store()\n524\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n525\t        except Exception as e:\n526\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n527\t\n528\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n529\t        self,\n530\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n531\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n532\t    ) -> dict:\n533\t        \"\"\"\n534\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n535\t\n536\t        Args:\n537\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n538\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n539\t                fetch_total_ms, and {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n549\t        )\n550\t\n551\t        try:\n552\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n553\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n554\t                classification.intent_type,\n555\t                classification.lookup_kind\n556\t            )\n557\t\n558\t            from ..environment.discovery location = entry.display_path\n571\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n572\t                else:\n573\t                    logger.info(f\"No )\n583\t\n584\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n585\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n586\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n587\t        cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n595\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n596\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n597\t                ),\n598\t            )\n599\t\n600\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n601\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n602\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n603\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n604\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n605\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n606\t            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n607\t\n608\t            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n609\t                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n610\t                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n611\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n612\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n613\t\n614\t                # Increment completed count\n615\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n616\t\n617\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n618\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n619\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n620\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n621\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n622\t                    total=total_sources,\n623\t                    source_name=source.value,\n624\t                    source_status=result.status,\n625\t                ))\n626\t\n627\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n628\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n629\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n630\t                timings.record(\n631\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n632\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n633\t                )\n634\t\n635\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n636\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n637\t                logger.error(\n638\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n639\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n640\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n641\t                )\n642\t\n643\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n644\t                failed_sources = []\n645\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n646\t                    failed_sources.append({\n647\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n648\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n649\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n650\t                    })\n651\t\n652\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n653\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n654\t                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n655\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n656\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n657\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n658\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n659\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n660\t                )\n661\t\n662\t                # Update intent status to failed\n663\t                store = get_store()\n664\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n665\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n666\t                    \"failed\",\n667\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n668\t                )\n669\t\n670\t                return {\n671\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n672\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n673\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n674\t                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n675\t                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n676\t                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n677\t                }\n678\t\n679\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n680\t            required_sources_failed = [\n681\t                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n682\t                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n683\t                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n684\t            ]\n685\t\n686\t            if sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n689\t\n690\t                # Handle terminal failure\n691\t                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n692\t                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n693\t                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n694\t                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n695\t                )\n696\t\n697\t                return {\n698\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n699\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n700\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n701\t                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n702\t                    \"message\":   intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n708\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n709\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n710\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n711\t                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n712\t                urgency=classification.urgency,\n713\t            )\n714\t\n715\t            synth_start = timings.clock()\n716\t            try:\n717\t                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n718\t            except Exception as synth_e:\n719\t                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n720\t                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n721\t                logger.error(\n722\t                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n723\t                )\n724\t\n725\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n726\t                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n727\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n728\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n729\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n730\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n731\t                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n732\t                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n733\t                )\n734\t\n735\t                # Update intent status to failed\n736\t                store = get_store()\n737\t                await         \"status\": \"failed\",\n747\t                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n748\t                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n749\t                    \"degraded\": True,\n750\t                }\n751\t\n752\t            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n753\t            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n758\t            store = get_store()\n759\t            _topic_type_map = {\n760\t                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n761\t                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n762\t            }\n763\t            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n764\t            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n765\t                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n766\t                topic_type=topic_type,\n767\t                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n768\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n769\t            )\n770\t            await lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n777\t            )\n778\t\n779\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n780\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n781\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n782\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n783\t                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n784\t                data=synthesize_result.data,\n785\t                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n786\t                result_type=result_type,\n787\t            )\n788\t\n789\t            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no        }\n818\t\n819\t        except Exception as e:\n820\t            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n821\t\n822\t            # Detect terminal \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n835\t            }\n836\t\n837\t    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n838\t        self,\n839\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n840\t FetchIntentType:\n878\t        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n879\t\n880\t        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n881\t        \"\"\"\n882\t        # Map enum values by string\n883\t        type_map = {\n884\t            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n885\t            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n886\t            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n887\t            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n888\t            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n889\t            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n890\t            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n891\t            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n892\t        }\n893\t\n894\t        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n895\t        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n896\t            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n897\t                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n898\t            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n899\t                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n900\t            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n901\t                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n902\t\n903\t        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n904\t\n905\t    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n906\t        self,\n907\t        bead_ref: str,\n908\t    ) -> dict | None:\n909\t        \"\"\"\n910\t        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n911\t\n912\t        Args:\n913\t            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n914\t\n915\t intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call quota exhausted.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n        self.parse_error = parse_error\n        self.raw_output = raw_output\n        self.retry_count = retry_count\n        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n\n        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n\n        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self._load_router_prompt()\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n        # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            # Measure ZAI proxy call time\n            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=256,\n                temperature=0.0,\n            )\n            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Measure JSON parsing time\n            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n            try:\n                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n            except ParseLLMError as e:\n                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n                raise e\n            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n\n            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n            logger.info(\n                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"total_estimate_ms={proxy_ms + parse_ms:.2f}\"\n            )\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except ParseLLMError as e:\n            \"\"\"\n            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n\n            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n            \"\"\"\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n                # Increment completed count\n                completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n
